  373. ^ "@Spotify now available in Andorra, Ireland, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg and Monaco". 13 November 2012. Archived from the original on 23 February 2020. Retrieved 11 January 2018 – via Twitter.
  374. ^ Brian, Matt (11 February 2013). "Spotify Launches in Italy, Portugal and Poland". TNW. Retrieved 4 May 2024.
  375. ^ "Spotify begins Latin America push with Mexico launch". BBC News. 16 April 2013. Archived from the original on 21 April 2019. Retrieved 7 November 2016.
  376. ^ Russell, Jon (24 September 2013). "Spotify is now live in 32 countries after launching in Taiwan, Argentina, Greece and Turkey". The Next Web. Archived from the original on 16 December 2019. Retrieved 7 November 2016.
  377. ^ Kastrenakes, Jacob (11 December 2013). "Spotify launches in 20 new markets throughout Latin America and Europe". The Verge. Archived from the original on 21 April 2019. Retrieved 7 November 2016.
  378. ^ Dimaculangan, Archie (9 April 2014). "Spotify says 'Mabuhay' to Filipino music lovers". Rappler. Retrieved 4 May 2024.
  379. ^ "Spotify opens to the important Brazilian market". RAIN News. 28 May 2014. Retrieved 4 May 2024.
  380. ^ Edmonds, Rich (30 September 2014). "The wait is over, Spotify finally launches in Canada!". Windows Central. Retrieved 4 May 2024.
  381. ^ "Spotify makes long-awaited entry into Indonesia". The Jakarta Post. 31 March 2016. Archived from the original on 12 August 2017. Retrieved 22 August 2017.
  382. ^ Kikuchi, Daisuke (29 September 2016). "Spotify finally launches in Japan, a nation where other music streaming services have struggled". The Japan Times. Retrieved 7 May 2024.
  383. ^ Byford, Sam (29 September 2016). "Spotify's mobile lyrics feature is launching first in Japan". The Verge. Retrieved 7 May 2024.
  384. ^ "Spotify to launch in Thailand on 22 August". The Nation. 11 August 2016. Archived from the original on 22 June 2019. Retrieved 22 August 2017.
  385. ^ Russell, Jon (21 August 2017). "Spotify launches in Thailand to continue its Asia push". TechCrunch. Archived from the original on 21 April 2019. Retrieved 7 December 2017.
  386. ^ Stacy.Goldrick@groupsjr.com (13 March 2023). "South Africa Looks Back at Five Years of Spotify Streaming". Spotify. Retrieved 7 May 2024.
  387. ^ יבלונקו, יסמין (12 March 2018). "ברוכה הבאה: ספוטיפיי הגיעה לישראל; המחיר – 20 ש' לחודש". Globes (in Hebrew). Retrieved 7 May 2024.
  388. ^ "Spotify este disponibil şi în România". Descopera (in Romanian). 13 March 2018. Retrieved 7 May 2024.
  389. ^ "Spotify chính thức ra mắt tại Việt Nam: cung cấp hơn 35 triệu bài hát quốc tế và nhạc Việt, người dùng miễn phí vẫn có thể truy cập đầy đủ kho nhạc". genk.vn (in Vietnamese). Retrieved 7 May 2024.
  390. ^ "Spotify Launches in the Middle East and North Africa". Variety. 13 November 2018. Archived from the original on 19 December 2019. Retrieved 14 November 2018.
  391. ^ "Spotify finally takes off in UAE and North Africa after 'soft' launch". Music Biz Nation. Archived from the original on 7 August 2020. Retrieved 18 April 2020.
  392. ^ "Spotify Now Available in India, Apps Show Up on App Store, Google Play". NDTV India. 26 February 2019. Archived from the original on 16 December 2019. Retrieved 26 February 2019.
  393. ^ "Spotify Is Now Available in Russia, Croatia, Ukraine, and 10 Other European Markets". 14 July 2020. Archived from the original on 14 July 2020. Retrieved 14 July 2020.
  394. ^ "Spotify Launches in South Korea". Spotify. 1 February 2021. Retrieved 2 February 2021.
  395. ^ "Spotify set to come to Bangladesh". The Daily Star. 24 February 2021. Retrieved 29 March 2021.
  396. ^ a b c d "Spotify Expands International Footprint, Bringing Audio to 80+ New Markets". 22 February 2021. Retrieved 23 February 2021.

  417. ^ "Spotify Is Burying Musicians for Their Apple Deals". Bloomberg. 26 August 2016. Retrieved 22 July 2020.

  421. ^ "Spotify Pivots on Global Cultures Initiative, Alarming Music Industry". Rolling Stone. 4 October 2019. Retrieved 18 February 2020.

  427. ^ Yang, Maya (26 January 2022). "Spotify removes Neil Young music in feud over Joe Rogan's false Covid claims". The Guardian. Retrieved 27 January 2022.
  428. ^ Shafer, Ellise (28 January 2022). "Joni Mitchell Says She's Removing Music From Spotify: 'Irresponsible People Are Spreading Lies'". Variety.
  429. ^ Repko, Melissa (30 January 2022). "Bruce Springsteen guitarist Nils Lofgren joins protest of Spotify over Covid misinformation". CNBC. Retrieved 30 January 2022.

  431. ^ "David Crosby, Graham Nash and Stephen Stills ask to pull their content from Spotify". NPR. Retrieved 5 February 2022.

Further reading

[edit]
  • Eriksson, Maria et al. (2019). Spotify Teardown: Inside the Black Box of Streaming Music. The MIT Press. ISBN 978-0262038904.
  • Pelly, Liz (2025). Mood Machine: The Rise of Spotify and the Costs of the Perfect Playlist. New York: Atria/One Signal Publishers. ISBN 9781668083505. OCLC 1438666130.
  • Šmite, Darja; Moe, Nils Brede; Floryan, Marcin; Gonzalez-Huerta, Javier; Dorner, Michael; Sablis, Aivars (June 2023). "Decentralized Decision-making and Scaled Autonomy at Spotify". Journal of Systems and Software. 200 111649. doi:10.1016/j.jss.2023.111649. hdl:11250/3131849. ISSN 0164-1212. S2CID 256931802.
